text_blocks.text: One key figure in the plans for what
would come to be known as American Empire,
was a geographer named Isiah Bowman. Bowman
was the director of the American Geographical
Society in 1914. Three years later in 1917, he was
appointed to then President Woodrow Wilson's
inquiry in 1917. The inquiry was the idea of
President Wilson and the American delegation
from the Paris Peace Conference. The point of this
inquiry was to build a premise that would allow
for U.S authorship of a 'new world' which was to
be characterized by geographical order. As a
result of his role in the inquiry, Isiah Bowman
would come to be known as Wilson's geographer.
